title: Ansible の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article demonstrates high factual accuracy, with technical claims aligning with official Ansible documentation and widely accepted best practices.
It provides comprehensive coverage of common causes for Ansible's 401 errors, including SSH authentication failures, sudo password mismatches, and incorrect API credentials.
The suggested solutions are practical, offering clear code examples and command-line options that directly address the identified issues.
The article promotes strong security practices by emphasizing the use of Ansible Vault for sensitive data.
The content is largely current, reflecting recent discussions and documentation within the Ansible ecosystem.

sources.title: How to Fix Ansible Authentication failure Errors
sources.claim: When an authentication error occurs in Ansible, it indicates a failure to authenticate to the target host or a linked external service.

sources.title: How to Fix Ansible Authentication failure Errors
sources.claim: This error mainly occurs due to authentication failure during SSH connection, password mismatch during sudo privilege escalation, or incorrect API tokens or credentials, leading to a critical situation where Playbook execution stops midway.

sources.claim: Occurs when the private key used for SSH does not correspond to the target host's public key, or when Ansible cannot handle a passphrase-protected key.

sources.title: Ansibleが理解できない理由はLinuxにあった【OS編】第3回 ：AnsibleのSSH接続が失敗すると UNREACHABLE エラーになる。（Connection refused / Permission denied / Timeout など） - Qiita

sources.title: Understanding privilege escalation: become — Ansible Community Documentation
sources.claim: When executing tasks requiring administrator privileges on the target host (e.g., package installation, file editing), if `become: yes` is set but `become_password` is not, sudo authentication will fail.

sources.title: How to Use Ansible become for sudo Privilege Escalation
sources.claim: When executing tasks requiring administrator privileges on the target host (e.g., package installation, file editing), if `become: yes` is set but `become_password` is not, sudo authentication will fail.

sources.title: Sudo no longer works without become password · Issue #85835 - GitHub
sources.claim: When executing tasks requiring administrator privileges on the target host (e.g., package installation, file editing), if `become: yes` is set but `become_password` is not, sudo authentication will fail.

sources.title: Api Token authentication issue with URI module #38120 - GitHub
sources.claim: If API tokens, access keys, or passwords used in modules like `uri`, `ansible.posix.synchronize`, or cloud provider integration modules (e.g., aws_s3, azure_vm) are incorrect, the external service responds with 401 Unauthorized, and the module fails.

sources.title: How can I implement ansible with per-host passwords, securely? - Server Fault
sources.claim: If parallelism is limited by the `-f` option, authentication for multiple hosts occurs simultaneously. In cases where authentication information differs per host, it is recommended to verify with a single execution before moving to parallel execution.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: Integrate direct citations to official Ansible documentation (e.g., docs.ansible.com) for core technical claims to enhance authority.
Optional improvement: Explicitly state the Ansible version(s) for which the advice is most applicable to provide clearer context, given potential version-specific behaviors.

Optional improvement: Consider adding more detailed guidance or a dedicated section on managing passphrase-protected SSH keys with Ansible (e.g., using `ssh-add` or `ansible_ssh_common_args` with `ssh-agent`).
